originalTitle: Nintendo will stop selling the original Switch in Europe next year
summary: Nintendo is making a new version of the Switch 2 with a replaceable battery in Europe - but its predecessor has a very different future. As part of an updated FAQ about revisions to Nintendo hardware in Europe, the company confirmed that it will stop selling all iterations of the original Switch on the continent […]
content: Nintendo is making a new version of the Switch 2 with a replaceable battery in Europe — but its predecessor has a very different future. As part of an updated FAQ about revisions to Nintendo hardware in Europe, the company confirmed that it will stop selling all iterations of the original Switch on the continent starting next year. Here’s the full statement: > From mid-February 2027, almost ten years after Nintendo Switch launched in March 2017, Nintendo will no longer sell to retailers hardware in the Nintendo Switch family of systems — specifically Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch Lite, and Nintendo Switch – OLED Model. Sales of Nintendo Switch hardware on Nintendo Store will also end in mid-February 2027. The news comes as Nintendo is making a bunch of changes to the rest of its lineup due to EU regulations requiring user-replaceable batteries. Starting this summer, the company says it will start introducing updated versions of various devices on “a rolling basis,” ahead of the regulations coming into effect on February 18th, 2027. “There is no difference in functionality between current products and revised products containing user-replaceable batteries,” Nintendo says. The Switch 2 is the most notable product being updated — the new version is expected to start rolling out in the fall — but there will also be versions of the Joy-Con controllers, Joy-Con 2, Switch 2 Pro Controller, and N64 and GameCube Switch controllers with user-replaceable batteries. “Due to a variety of factors, revised products may not become available in all European countries simultaneously,” Nintendo notes. It’s currently unclear what fate might await the original Switch in other territories, but the decade-old hardware is still getting notable first-party game releases, including Rhythm Heaven Grove and Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream.
chineseTitle: 任天堂将于明年停止在欧洲销售初代 Switch
chineseSummary: 任天堂正在欧洲生产带有可更换电池的新版 Switch 2，但其前身的未来却截然不同。作为有关欧洲任天堂硬件修订的更新常见问题解答的一部分，该公司确认将停止在欧洲大陆销售所有原始 Switch 版本 [...]
chineseContent: 任天堂正在欧洲生产带有可更换电池的新版 Switch 2，但其前身的未来却截然不同。作为有关欧洲任天堂硬件修订的最新常见问题解答的一部分，该公司确认将从明年开始在欧洲大陆停止销售所有版本的初代 Switch。完整声明如下： > 从 2027 年 2 月中旬开始，也就是 2017 年 3 月推出 Nintendo Switch 近十年后，任天堂将不再向零售商销售 Nintendo Switch 系列系统中的硬件，特别是 Nintendo Switch、Nintendo Switch Lite 和 Nintendo Switch – OLED 型号。 Nintendo Store 上 Nintendo Switch 硬件的销售也将于 2027 年 2 月中旬结束。此消息发布之际，由于欧盟法规要求用户更换电池，任天堂正在对其其余产品阵容进行一系列更改。该公司表示，从今年夏天开始，将在 2027 年 2 月 18 日法规生效之前开始“滚动”推出各种设备的更新版本。“当前产品和包含用户可更换电池的修订产品之间的功能没有区别，”任天堂表示。 Switch 2 是最引人注目的更新产品——新版本预计将于秋季开始推出——但也会有 Joy-Con 控制器、Joy-Con 2、Switch 2 Pro 控制器以及带有用户可更换电池的 N64 和 GameCube Switch 控制器的版本。任天堂指出：“由于多种因素，修订后的产品可能无法同时在所有欧洲国家上市。”目前尚不清楚初代 Switch 在其他地区的命运会如何，但这款已有十年历史的硬件仍在发布著名的第一方游戏，包括 Rhythm Hea
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
